kernel_funcs

Classes

KernelFactory

Functions

adjust_sigma_n_bound(base_bounds[, true_noise_std, ...])

Adjust the log10 bounds of sigma_n based on known noise in the data.

_double_factorial(→ int)

Returns (2n-1)!! for n ≥ 0. By convention (-1)!! = 1.

matern_prefactor(r, nu[, length_scale])

Evaluate the polynomial prefactor P_p(z) * exp(-z)

Module Contents

kernel_funcs.adjust_sigma_n_bound(base_bounds, true_noise_std=None, cushion=0.5)[source]

Adjust the log10 bounds of sigma_n based on known noise in the data.

kernel_funcs._double_factorial(n: int) → int[source]

Returns (2n-1)!! for n ≥ 0. By convention (-1)!! = 1.

kernel_funcs.matern_prefactor(r, nu, length_scale=1.0)[source]

Evaluate the polynomial prefactor P_p(z) * exp(-z) for half‑integer ν = p + 0.5.

Parameters:

  • r (array_like) – Pairwise Euclidean distance(s).

  • nu (float) – Smoothness parameter (must be half‑integer).

  • length_scale (float, default 1.0) – Characteristic length ℓ.

Returns:

The value of exp(-z) * P_p(z) at each r.

Return type:

ndarray
